wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Imposter Game project overview section. Explain the game concept as a real-time multiplayer social deduction game where one player is secretly the imposter while others try to identify them. Document the project's purpose, target audience, and core value proposition. Include the complete technology stack (React 18 + Vite + Tailwind CSS for frontend, Node.js + Express + Socket.io for backend). Provide an architectural overview showing the client-server relationship and real-time communication flow. Explain the game mechanics, scoring system, and multi-platform accessibility. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of gameplay scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Setup and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the server setup and configuration components. Document the Express server initialization process, HTTP server creation, and Socket.IO configuration with CORS settings. Explain the server startup process, port configuration, and environment variable handling. Detail the middleware configuration including CORS policy, JSON parsing, and security considerations. Include server lifecycle management, graceful shutdown procedures, and error handling strategies. Document the health check endpoint implementation and monitoring capabilities. Address production deployment considerations, scaling strategies, and performance optimization techniques for the server component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Home Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Home screen component. Document the room creation and joining functionality including form validation, input sanitization, and error handling. Explain the animated background elements with floating emojis and glow effects. Detail the responsive design implementation, button interactions, and state management for mode switching between create/join modes. Include the connection status indicators, error messaging system, and keyboard navigation support. Document the styling approach using Tailwind CSS classes and custom animations. Provide examples of form validation logic and user interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application structure and component hierarchy. Document the main App.jsx component as the root component and its screen routing system. Explain the component lifecycle, prop drilling patterns, and how different game screens are organized and rendered. Detail the screen-based architecture where each game phase has its own dedicated component (Home, Lobby, RoleReveal, CluePhase, Discussion, Voting, Results). Include component composition patterns, conditional rendering based on game state, and the overall application flow from initial load to game completion. Document how components communicate with each other and the GameContext provider.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Imposter Game. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js, npm), step-by-step installation instructions for both server and client, and environment configuration. Document the development setup process including local server startup, client development server, and hot reload configuration. Provide clear instructions for accessing the game locally and testing the real-time functionality. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Address both manual setup and automated deployment options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the GameManager class and its role in managing game state throughout the Imposter Game. Document the room lifecycle management including room creation, player joining, and room deletion processes. Explain the player state tracking system, including connection status, role assignments, and score management. Detail the game phase transitions from lobby to role reveal, clue phase, discussion, voting, and results phases. Document the timer management system for coordinating game timing across all phases. Include the scoring algorithm implementation, vote tallying mechanism, and round progression logic. Address error handling, state validation, and recovery mechanisms for disconnected players. Explain the serialization methods used for client-server communication and state synchronization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lobby Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Lobby screen component. Document the player list management system, host controls, and game setup options. Explain the player status indicators, host designation display, and real-time player updates. Detail the game start functionality, room code display, and player removal mechanisms. Include the responsive layout for player cards, hover interactions, and visual feedback systems. Document the integration with GameContext for state synchronization and the socket event handling for lobby updates. Provide examples of player management operations and host privilege handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management and GameContext
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the centralized state management system using React Context. Explain the GameContext provider pattern and how it manages the complete game state including rooms, players, current phase, timers, and scores. Document the context value structure, action creators for state mutations, and how components consume context data. Detail the state synchronization with the server, error handling for state updates, and performance optimizations like context splitting. Include examples of how different components access and modify state, subscription patterns, and best practices for avoiding unnecessary re-renders. Address state persistence during reconnections and graceful degradation when server communication fails.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Imposter Game system. Describe the high-level design showing the client-server architecture with real-time WebSocket communication. Document the component interactions between React frontend and Node.js backend, explaining how Socket.IO events coordinate game state synchronization. Include system boundaries, data flows, and integration patterns between the client-side React application and server-side game logic. Explain the technical decisions behind the event-driven architecture, state management approach using React Context, and real-time communication patterns. Address cross-cutting concerns like connection handling, error recovery, and graceful degradation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Topics and Content System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the topics and content management system used in the Imposter Game. Document the topic category structure including general, family, and adult categories with their respective word lists and difficulty levels. Explain the random topic selection algorithm and how topics are assigned to players during game setup. Detail the content validation and sanitization processes to ensure appropriate content for different categories. Document the extensibility mechanisms for adding new topics, categories, and custom content. Include guidelines for content moderation, age-appropriate filtering, and cultural sensitivity considerations. Address the relationship between topic categories and game mechanics, including how different categories affect gameplay dynamics and player experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role Reveal Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Role Reveal screen component. Document the private role display mechanism, timing controls, and transition animations. Explain the role card design, visual styling for different roles, and the countdown timer implementation. Detail the screen transition logic, automatic advancement to next phase, and user interaction handling during the reveal period. Include the responsive design considerations, animation sequences, and accessibility features for role display. Document the integration with game state management and the socket events for role distribution.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Integration and useSocket Hook
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for WebSocket integration using the useSocket hook. Explain the connection management, event handling patterns, and automatic reconnection logic. Document all Socket.IO events used in the application including room creation/joining, game state updates, player actions, and error handling. Detail the hook's implementation of connection lifecycle management, error recovery strategies, and graceful disconnection handling. Include examples of event listeners, message formatting, and how the hook integrates with React components. Address performance considerations for real-time communication, connection pooling, and memory management for long-lived connections. Document debugging techniques and common connection issues with their solutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive server architecture documentation for the Imposter Game backend. Document the Express server initialization, Socket.IO configuration, and CORS settings. Explain the GameManager class structure and its role in managing game state, room lifecycle, and player interactions. Detail the topics.js implementation for managing word categories and game content. Include server-side event handling, real-time communication protocols, and state synchronization mechanisms. Document the REST endpoint for health checks and WebSocket connection management. Address security considerations, error handling, and performance optimization strategies for the server component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Socket Events and API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all Socket.IO events and WebSocket communication protocols used in the Imposter Game server. Document all client-to-server events including createRoom, joinRoom, startGame, submitClue, submitVote, imposterGuess, and disconnect with their payload structures, validation requirements, and error responses. Detail all server-to-client events such as roomCreated, joinedRoom, phaseChanged, timerTick, clueReceived, voteSubmitted, and game state updates with their data schemas and timing. Include event flow diagrams showing typical game sequences and state transitions. Document the real-time communication patterns, event ordering guarantees, and synchronization mechanisms. Address error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and connection recovery procedures. Provide examples of event payloads, response formats, and integration patterns for client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Clue Phase Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Clue Phase screen component. Document the timed clue submission interface, real-time clue broadcasting, and timer synchronization. Explain the clue input validation, character limits, and submission mechanics. Detail the clue display system, real-time updates from other players, and the countdown timer integration. Include the responsive design for clue cards, input field styling, and visual feedback for submissions. Document the socket event handling for clue updates, timer management, and the transition to next game phase. Provide examples of clue validation logic and real-time synchronization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Configuration and Development Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the complete build configuration and development setup for the React client application. Explain the Vite configuration including development server settings, build optimization, and asset handling. Detail the package.json dependencies covering React, Socket.IO client, Canvas Confetti, and build tools. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration including design system setup, color palette, responsive breakpoints, and custom utilities. Include PostCSS configuration for CSS processing and optimization. Provide step-by-step development setup instructions, environment variable configuration, and deployment preparation. Address build performance optimization, bundle analysis, and production deployment strategies. Include troubleshooting common build issues and development workflow best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive client architecture documentation for the React-based frontend application. Document the component hierarchy starting from App.jsx and the screen routing system. Explain the centralized state management approach using React Context and the GameContext provider pattern. Detail the WebSocket integration through the useSocket hook and real-time event handling. Include the Vite build configuration, Tailwind CSS styling approach, and responsive design implementation. Document the component lifecycle, state synchronization with the server, and error handling strategies. Address performance considerations, memory management, and client-side optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Discussion Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Discussion screen component. Document the shared clue review interface, discussion timer, and player interaction features. Explain the clue display system, player attribution, and real-time discussion mechanics. Detail the timer integration, phase transitions, and user engagement features. Include the responsive layout for clue cards, scrolling behavior, and visual hierarchy for discussion content. Document the socket event handling for clue sharing, timer synchronization, and the integration with GameContext for state management. Provide examples of discussion flow and player interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game Screens and UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for all game screens and UI components in the Imposter Game. Document each screen component including Home (room creation/joining), Lobby (player management), RoleReveal (private role display), CluePhase (timed clue submission), Discussion (shared clue review), Voting (target selection), and Results (scoreboard display). For each screen, describe the visual appearance, user interaction patterns, component props, state management, and integration with the global GameContext. Include responsive design considerations, animation implementations, and accessibility features. Document the screen transition logic and how each component contributes to the overall game flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voting Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Voting screen component. Document the target selection interface, vote submission mechanics, and real-time voting updates. Explain the player targeting system, vote validation, and submission confirmation. Detail the voting timer integration, player status indicators, and the visual feedback for selected targets. Include the responsive design for player cards, hover states, and selection animations. Document the socket event handling for vote submissions, real-time vote counting, and the integration with game state management. Provide examples of voting logic and real-time synchronization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Socket Events and API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Socket.IO event system used in Imposter Game. Document all client-to-server events including createRoom, joinRoom, startGame, submitClue, submitVote, imposterGuess, nextRound, playAgain, and reconnect. For each event, specify the data payload format, expected responses, and error conditions. Document all server-to-client events including timerTick, phaseChanged, roundResult, gameOver, playerDisconnected, and playerReconnected. Include event timing, frequency, and state synchronization patterns. Provide practical examples of event handling in both client and server contexts, error handling strategies, and debugging approaches for WebSocket communication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Results Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Results screen component. Document the round results display, score calculation, and winner announcement system. Explain the scoreboard layout, individual player scores, and team performance indicators. Detail the animation systems for result presentation, transition to next round, and game over scenarios. Include the responsive design for score displays, visual feedback for winners, and the integration with GameContext for final standings. Document the socket event handling for result broadcasts, score updates, and the transition logic to final results or next round. Provide examples of scoring calculations and result presentation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game Mechanics and Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the game mechanics and rule implementation in Imposter Game. Document the complete game flow including room management, player assignment, role distribution, and phase transitions. Explain the scoring system with detailed point allocation (+2 for correct votes, +3 for surviving imposter, +1 consolation for imposter guesses). Detail the timer management across different game phases (Role Reveal: 10s, Clue Phase: 60s, Discussion: 60s, Voting: 45s). Document the topic selection system with three categories (General, Family, Adult) and word pool management. Include tie-breaking rules, host rotation, and player reconnection handling. Address edge cases, error conditions, and state synchronization challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive styling and design system documentation for the Imposter Game frontend. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration including color palette, spacing scale, typography system, and responsive breakpoints. Explain the design system approach with consistent styling patterns, component styling strategies, and theme customization options. Detail the animation and transition implementations using CSS transitions and canvas-confetti effects. Include responsive design patterns, mobile-first approach, and cross-device compatibility considerations. Document the PostCSS configuration, build-time optimizations, and performance considerations for the styling system. Address accessibility features, dark mode support, and brand consistency throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development and Build Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development and build process documentation for the Imposter Game. Document the Vite build configuration including development server setup, hot reload configuration, and production build optimization. Explain the npm script setup for both development and production environments. Detail the Tailwind CSS integration, PostCSS processing, and asset optimization strategies. Include environment variable configuration for both client and server, deployment preparation, and build artifact management. Document the development workflow including code splitting, lazy loading, and performance monitoring. Address build troubleshooting, dependency management, and continuous integration considerations for both client and server components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Hosting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and hosting documentation for the Imposter Game. Document the complete deployment process for both server and client components, including Railway for the server and Vercel for the client. Explain environment variable configuration, domain setup, and SSL certificate management. Detail the build process for production deployments, including asset optimization and CDN configuration. Include monitoring setup, logging configuration, and performance optimization strategies. Document rollback procedures, update workflows, and maintenance tasks. Address scaling considerations, load balancing, and cost optimization for hosting providers. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and platform-specific configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for the Imposter Game. Document common connection issues including WebSocket connection failures, server availability problems, and client-side connectivity errors. Address game state synchronization issues, timer desynchronization, and phase transition problems. Include debugging guides for both client and server-side issues, logging strategies, and diagnostic tools. Document performance troubleshooting including memory leaks, connection pooling, and resource optimization. Provide solutions for browser compatibility issues, mobile device problems, and network connectivity challenges. Include frequently asked questions about game rules, technical requirements, and usage scenarios with clear, actionable answers.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Imposter Game: Real-Time Multiplayer Party Game

## Project Introduction

Imposter Game is a real-time multiplayer party game built with modern web technologies, designed to recreate the social deduction experience of the popular Among Us game. The project features a complete client-server architecture with WebSocket-based real-time communication, allowing players to collaborate and compete in rounds of clue-giving, discussion, and voting.

### Purpose Statement
The game provides an engaging social deduction experience where one player secretly acts as the "imposter" while others try to identify them through strategic clue-giving and discussion. The game emphasizes real-time interaction, timing-based gameplay mechanics, and social deduction elements.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Synchronization**: Maintain consistent game state across all connected players
- **Social Deduction Gameplay**: Implement classic imposter/victim dynamics with strategic clue-giving
- **Cross-platform Compatibility**: Support multiple devices and browsers seamlessly
- **Robust State Management**: Handle complex game phases with proper state transitions
- **Graceful Degradation**: Support player reconnection and temporary disconnections

### Target Audience
- Casual gamers seeking social deduction experiences
- Friends and family groups looking for interactive entertainment
- Developers interested in real-time multiplayer game architecture
- Educational contexts for teaching social deduction and communication skills

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a clean client-server architecture with clear separation of concerns between the frontend React application and the Node.js backend server.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Application] --> B[Socket.IO Client]
    B --> C[Express Server]
    C --> D[Socket.IO Server]
    D --> E[Game Manager]
    E --> F[Room Management]
    E --> G[State Machine]
    E --> H[Timer System]
    E --> I[Scoring System]
    
    J[Topics Database] --> E
    K[React Context] --> A
    L[UI Components] --> A
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture:**
- **React 18** with functional components and hooks
- **Socket.IO Client** for real-time bidirectional communication
- **Tailwind CSS** for responsive styling and animations
- **Game Context** for centralized state management
- **Component-based UI** with phase-specific screens

**Backend Architecture:**
- **Node.js + Express** HTTP server foundation
- **Socket.IO** WebSocket implementation for real-time features
- **Game Manager** central state machine and business logic
- **Room Management** individual game session handling
- **Timer System** coordinated countdown mechanisms

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

1. **Observer Pattern**: Socket.IO events notify components of state changes
2. **State Machine Pattern**: Game phases transition based on defined rules
3. **Factory Pattern**: Room creation and player management
4. **Singleton Pattern**: Global game manager instance
5. **Context Pattern**: React context for state sharing across components

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Client Browser
    participant Socket as Socket.IO
    participant Server as Game Server
    participant Manager as Game Manager
    participant Room as Room Instance
    
    Client->>+Socket: connect()
    Socket->>+Server: WebSocket handshake
    Server->>+Manager: initialize connection
    Manager->>+Room: create room instance
    Room-->>-Manager: room ready
    Manager-->>-Server: room created
    Server-->>-Socket: connection established
    Socket-->>-Client: connected
    
    Client->>+Socket: createRoom({name})
    Socket->>+Server: createRoom event
    Server->>+Manager: createRoom()
    Manager->>+Room: create room
    Room-->>-Manager: room data
    Manager-->>-Server: room created
    Server-->>-Socket: roomCreated event
    Socket-->>-Client: roomCreated data
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Server Entry Point** (`server/index.js`)
- Express server initialization with CORS support
- Socket.IO server configuration with real-time event handling
- Complete game flow orchestration and phase management
- Player lifecycle management including reconnection handling

**Client Entry Point** (`client/src/main.jsx`)
- React application bootstrap with provider wrapping
- Game context provider for state management
- Root component rendering with Tailwind CSS styling

### Core Modules

**Game Manager** (`server/gameManager.js`)
- Central state machine managing all game logic
- Room lifecycle and player management
- Phase-based game progression with validation
- Scoring system and tie-breaking algorithms
- Timer coordination across all connected players

**Socket Communication** (`client/src/hooks/useSocket.js`)
- WebSocket connection management with automatic reconnection
- Server URL configuration with environment variables
- Connection state tracking and error handling
- Reconnection logic for seamless user experience

**Game Context** (`client/src/context/GameContext.jsx`)
- Centralized state management for all game data
- Action creators for game operations
- Event listener registration and cleanup
- User interface state synchronization

### Configuration Approach

**Server Configuration** (`server/package.json`)
- Node.js dependencies: Express, Socket.IO, CORS
- Development scripts for hot reloading during development
- Production deployment configuration

**Client Configuration** (`client/package.json`)
- React 18 with modern ES modules
- Vite build system for fast development
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- Socket.IO client for real-time communication

**Build Configuration** (`client/vite.config.js`)
- Development server settings with proxy configuration
- Asset optimization and bundling
- Environment variable handling

**Styling Configuration** (`client/tailwind.config.js`)
- Custom color palette with dark theme support
- Animation utilities for smooth transitions
- Responsive design breakpoints
- Custom gradient configurations

### External Dependencies

**Server Dependencies:**
- `express`: Web server framework
- `socket.io`: Real-time bidirectional communication
- `cors`: Cross-origin resource sharing support

**Client Dependencies:**
- `react`: Component-based UI library
- `react-dom`: React DOM rendering
- `socket.io-client`: WebSocket client library
- `canvas-confetti`: Visual effects for celebrations

**Development Dependencies:**
- `@vitejs/plugin-react`: React plugin for Vite
- `tailwindcss`: Utility-first CSS framework
- `autoprefixer`: PostCSS autoprefixing
- `vite`: Modern build tool

### Integration Points

**Real-time Communication Flow:**
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Client Component] --> B[Game Context Action]
    B --> C[Socket Event]
    C --> D[Server Handler]
    D --> E[Game Manager]
    E --> F[Room State Update]
    F --> G[Socket Broadcast]
    G --> H[Client Event Listener]
    H --> I[UI Update]
```

**State Synchronization:**
- Bidirectional state updates through Socket.IO events
- Client-side state caching for offline resilience
- Server-side authoritative state management
- Consistent state across all connected clients

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App Component] --> B[Game Context Provider]
    B --> C[Home Screen]
    B --> D[Lobby Screen]
    B --> E[Game Screens]
    
    E --> F[Role Reveal]
    E --> G[Clue Phase]
    E --> H[Discussion]
    E --> I[Voting]
    E --> J[Results]
    
    F --> K[Private Role Info]
    G --> L[Public Clue Sharing]
    H --> M[All Clue Display]
    I --> N[Vote Collection]
    J --> O[Score Calculation]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multiplayer Game Sessions:**
- Room-based game sessions with unique 4-character codes
- Up to 8 players per room with dynamic capacity
- Host-based room management with automatic host rotation
- Real-time player presence and connection status tracking

**Game Phases:**
- **Role Reveal**: Private role assignment with 10-second reveal timer
- **Clue Phase**: 60-second timed clue submission with immediate broadcasting
- **Discussion Phase**: Shared clue viewing and 60-second discussion period
- **Voting Phase**: 45-second voting with instant feedback
- **Results Phase**: Round results with scoring and elimination

**Advanced Features:**
- **Imposter Guessing**: Secret topic guessing with consolation points
- **Player Reconnection**: Graceful handling of network interruptions
- **Automatic Timing**: Coordinated countdown timers across all clients
- **Dynamic Scoring**: Complex scoring system with tie-breaking rules
- **Visual Feedback**: Animated transitions and real-time updates

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Synchronization** (`server/index.js`)
- WebSocket-based bidirectional communication
- Event-driven architecture with proper error handling
- Automatic room cleanup and memory management
- Graceful degradation for disconnected players

**State Management** (`client/src/context/GameContext.jsx`)
- Centralized state with React Context API
- Action-based state updates with proper validation
- Error boundary implementation with user feedback
- Session persistence across browser refreshes

**UI/UX Design** (`client/src/screens/`)
- Component-based screen architecture
- Smooth transitions between game phases
- Responsive design for mobile and desktop
- Visual feedback for all user interactions

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Home
    Home --> Lobby: Create/Join Room
    Lobby --> RoleReveal: Start Game
    RoleReveal --> Clue: Begin Clue Phase
    Clue --> Discussion: Timer End
    Discussion --> Voting: Timer End
    Voting --> Results: Timer End
    Results --> RoleReveal: Next Round
    RoleReveal --> FinalResults: Game Over
    FinalResults --> Home: Play Again
    
    state RoleReveal {
        [*] --> PrivateRole
        PrivateRole --> PublicRole
    }
    
    state Results {
        [*] --> ScoreCalculation
        ScoreCalculation --> Elimination
        Elimination --> [*]
    }
```

**Performance Considerations:**
- Efficient WebSocket connection pooling
- Minimal state updates to reduce bandwidth
- Client-side caching for improved responsiveness
- Optimized rendering with React memoization

**Security Measures:**
- Input validation and sanitization
- Room code uniqueness enforcement
- Player authentication through socket IDs
- Rate limiting for game actions

**Scalability Features:**
- Modular architecture supporting horizontal scaling
- Stateless server design for load balancing
- Efficient memory management for room cleanup
- Configurable room limits and game parameters

---

**Sources:**
- [server/index.js](server/index.js)
- [server/gameManager.js](server/gameManager.js)
- [server/topics.js](server/topics.js)
- [client/src/App.jsx](client/src/App.jsx)
- [client/src/context/GameContext.jsx](client/src/context/GameContext.jsx)
- [client/src/hooks/useSocket.js](client/src/hooks/useSocket.js)
- [client/src/screens/Home.jsx](client/src/screens/Home.jsx)
- [client/src/screens/Lobby.jsx](client/src/screens/Lobby.jsx)
- [client/tailwind.config.js](client/tailwind.config.js)
- [client/package.json](client/package.json)
- [server/package.json](server/package.json)

</blog>
